reasoning:
The question asks whether the daily average temperature at Clermont-Ferrand Airport will be higher on three specific dates in late autumn/winter (Nov 2, Nov 25, Jan 24) than on a baseline date in mid-autumn (Oct 26). The expected daily average temperature at this location falls steadily during this period, from about ~11.0°C in late October, to ~9.5°C in early Nov, ~6.0°C in late Nov, and ~4.5°C in late Jan. Assuming the daily variations have standard deviations of roughly 4 to 5°C, the variance of the difference between any two independent days is about 32 (SD ~ 5.6°C). 
- For Nov 2 (7 days later), the expected drop is ~1.5°C. With SD ~ 5.6, Z is roughly -0.27, yielding a probability of ~40-42%.
- For Nov 25 (30 days later), the expected drop is ~5.0°C. With SD ~ 5.6 to 6.0, Z is roughly -0.83, yielding a probability of ~20-22%.
- For Jan 24 (90 days later), the expected drop is ~6.5°C. Though winter temperature variance is somewhat higher (SD of difference ~ 6.5°C), the mean difference sits around -1.0 standard deviations, yielding a probability of ~16%. 
We note the prompt's heuristic that 'uncertainty should increase and probabilities move closer to 0.5', but strong seasonal forcing strictly dictates that the deeper into winter we forecast, the less likely days are to be warmer than mostly mild October days, leading to decreasing probabilities.
